entities_added.label: Memory palace has 4 layers: Palace → Wing → Room → Locus
AI must translate questions to concerns before navigating rooms
Locus rank formula: inbound*2 + outbound + critical*10 + entry*3
Navigate palace by concern → room → purpose → core loci
Enable AI agents to navigate codebase without blind file scanning
Each room page contains Purpose, Core Loci, Traversal, Raw Paths

facts_written.statement: The memory palace is structured as a 4-layer navigable hierarchy: Palace (whole system), Wing (top-level module, 4 total), Room (functional cluster, 7 today), Locus (single promoted file).
facts_written.evidence: Palace — the whole system. One index page. Wing — a top-level module. 4 wings total. Room — a functional cluster inside a wing. 7 rooms today. Locus — a single file promoted into a room.

facts_written.statement: AI agents must first translate user questions into concerns, then navigate to matching rooms, verify ownership via purpose line, and only then access core loci files.
facts_written.evidence: 1. Translate the user's question into a concern. 2. Open the matching room page. 3. Read the purpose line — does this room actually own the concern? 4. Look at the core loci table.

facts_written.statement: Files are promoted into rooms based on a composite score of inbound references (×2), outbound references (×1), criticality (×10), and entry-point status (×3).
The prescribed action sequence for AI codebase navigation: identify concern, open matching room, verify ownership, read core loci table.
facts_written.evidence: 1. Translate the user's question into a concern. 2. Open the matching room page. 3. Read the purpose line. 4. Look at the core loci table.

facts_written.statement: The memory palace exists so AI agents can find relevant files through structured navigation rather than grep-scanning the entire codebase.
facts_written.evidence: The memory palace at 10_Wiki/palace/ is a navigable, room-based view of the codebase. This file tells AI agents how to use it.

facts_written.statement: Room pages follow a standardized structure with four sections that serve as navigation signals for AI agents.
facts_written.evidence: Purpose — one sentence naming the concern. Core Loci — ranked table. Traversal — links back to wing, palace, retrieval rules. Raw Paths — plaintext file paths.

entry_point_invoked.note: No standalone world model update module exists. Mapping performed inline using primitive observations.
